title: Urban Farming and Food Security
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) growing popularity of urban farming is changing how cities think about food production. Converting (17) unused spaces such as rooftops and vacant lots into gardens helps improve local food (18) sustainability. These projects also offer (19) educational opportunities for residents, teaching them about nutrition and seasonal produce.||Urban farms contribute to (20) environmental improvements by reducing food miles and carbon emissions. They also build a sense of (21) neighbourhood and help tackle food (22) insecurity in deprived areas. Local (23) government is increasingly supportive, offering grants to encourage this form of (24) agricultural production.

example.text: The (0) GROWING popularity of urban farming is changing how cities think about food production. (GROW)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'unused' is formed by adding the prefix 'un-' to 'used'.
The noun 'sustainability' is formed from 'sustain' by adding the suffix '-ability'.
The adjective 'educational' is formed from 'educate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ational'.
The adjective 'environmental' is formed from 'environment' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'neighbourhood' is formed from 'neighbour' by adding the suffix '-hood'.
The noun 'insecurity' is formed from 'secure' by adding the prefix 'in-' and changing '-e' to '-ity'.
The noun 'government' is formed from 'govern' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'agricultural' is formed from 'agriculture' by replacing '-ure' with '-al'.
